&lt;---&gt; CAD</b><div><b style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(51, 51, 51);">Product Lifecycle&nbsp;</b></div><div><b style="background-color: transparent; color: rgb(51, 51, 51);">Example: Dishwasher&nbsp;</b></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="160" width="160" x="480" y="320" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="p1" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#f5f5f5;fontColor=#333333;strokeColor=#666666;align=center;" value="<div><span style="background-color: transparent; In her "Purchasing" role, she can directly see whether the individual components are externally procured or internally manufactured parts. This allows her to place the purchase orders and production orders correctly.</div></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="159" width="330" x="870" y="319" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="58EQvn1helDZmr70WJlO-2" edge="1" parent="1" source="58EQvn1helDZmr70WJlO-1" style="endArrow=classic;html=1;rounded=0;entryX=1;entryY=0.5;entryDx=0;entryDy=0;" target="p3" value=""> <mxGeometry transparent;">Who: Lena Müller (production planner / assembly supervisor)</span><div>Where: Final assembly line in the plant</div><div>What: Checking the tightening torques of the individual assemblies and assembling the sheet metal parts</div><div>How: An assembly drawing linked via the AAS and stored in the CAD model shows her the individual assembly steps and the corresponding tightening torques.</div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="130" width="330" x="870" y="510" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="58EQvn1helDZmr70WJlO-4" edge="1" parent="1" planner</div><div>What: Simplified CAD graphic of the dishwasher, configuration options</div><div>How: Access to the dishwasher’s STL file</div></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="100" width="280" x="-45" y="500" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="58EQvn1helDZmr70WJlO-7" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#f5f5f5;fontColor=#333333;strokeColor=#666666;align=center;" value="<div><span style="background-color: transparent;">Who: Sonja Musterfrau</span></div><div>Where: Customer Service</div><div>What: Identifying the defective part, retrieving the part number, and preparing a cost estimate</div><div>How: Viewing the overall assembly and subassemblies in a CAD program (indicating whether it is an externally procured assembly or an internally manufactured part), and displaying the purchasing BOM (Bill of Materials) with spare part prices</div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="145" width="290" x="-55" y="319" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell div.div: What: Creation of the CAD sheet metal parts for the dishwasher covers
How: Using the NX-CAD program in collaboration with the other designers and the manufacturing department

div.div: What: Procurement of external and internal parts for the dishwasher

How: Opening the machine in the AAS (Asset Administration Shell) and importing it into the NX-CAD program. In her "Purchasing" role, she can directly see whether the individual components are externally procured or internally manufactured parts. This allows her to place the purchase orders and production orders correctly.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 328px; height: 1px; padding-top: 417px; margin-left: 926px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #333333;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #333333; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.span: Who: Lena Müller (production planner / assembly supervisor)
div.div: Where: Final assembly line in the plant
What: Checking the tightening torques of the individual assemblies and assembling the sheet metal parts
How: An assembly drawing linked via the AAS and stored in the CAD model shows her the individual assembly steps and the corresponding tightening torques.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 278px; height: 1px; padding-top: 392px; margin-left: 11px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #333333;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #333333; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: What: Simplified CAD graphic of the dishwasher, configuration options
How: Access to the dishwasher’s STL file

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 288px; height: 1px; padding-top: 234px; margin-left: 1px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #333333;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #333333; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.div: What: Identifying the defective part, retrieving the part number, and preparing a cost estimate

How: Viewing the overall assembly and subassemblies in a CAD program (indicating whether it is an externally procured assembly or an internally manufactured part), and displaying the purchasing BOM (Bill of Materials) with spare part prices

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 293px; height: 1px; padding-top: 62px; margin-left: 1px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #333333;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #333333; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.p:
Who: Markus Mustermann
Where: Recycling sorting employee
What: Dismantling and sorting the individual components by material
How: Importing the AAS file into the program and displaying the assemblies with material information and disassembly instructions
